channel.title: Blog on Himalaya's Dev Journal
channel.description: Recent content in Blog on Himalaya's Dev Journal

item.title: Building a Game Engine from Scratch in Rust
item.description: Exploring ECS architecture, memory management, and why I chose Rust over C++ for my game engine project.

item.title: My Journey into Procedural Generation
item.description: How Perlin noise, cellular automata, and L-systems helped me create interesting-looking worlds for my game.

item.title: Why I Switched from VS Code to Neovim
item.description: A detailed comparison of my editor experience and why the terminal won out for my workflow.

item.title: Understanding WebAssembly for Game Development
item.description: How WASM lets me run C/C++ game code in the browser and what I learned porting my engine.

item.title: The Art of Writing Clean Game Code
item.description: Lessons from refactoring a spaghetti-code game project into something maintainable and fun to work on.

item.title: Setting Up CI/CD for a Hugo Blog
item.description: Automating builds, previews, and deployments for my personal site using GitHub Actions.

item.title: Learning Go Through CLI Tools
item.description: Building terminal utilities in Go — why the language’s simplicity made it a joy to learn.

item.title: Docker for Local Game Dev
item.description: Using containers to standardize my game development environment across machines.

item.title: My 2026 Reading List: Game Dev Books
item.description: Books that shaped my understanding of game architecture, design, and production.

item.title: Post-Mortem: Game Jam Entry
item.description: What went right, what went wrong, and what I’d do differently at my first game jam.

item.title: Optimizing WebGL Rendering for Mobile
item.description: Frametimes, draw calls, and texture atlases — how I got my game to run smoothly on a phone.

item.title: Why I Document Everything
item.description: The case for public learning logs and how writing things down accelerated my growth as a developer.

item.title: Scalable LLM Deployment on AWS with Ray Serve and FastAPI
item.description: A guide to deploying open-source LLMs using Ray Serve for orchestration and FastAPI for the serving layer on AWS infrastructure.
